questions.text: In our coffee shop example, a barista and customer were used to represent the client-server model. The barista represents the server and a customer represents the client. Which scenario BEST describes how the client-server model works in this analogy?

options.text: The customer takes a cup of coffee from a self-serve station without informing the barista.
The barista proactively prepares a coffee and brings it to the customer without being asked.
The customer makes their own coffee using the coffee shop equipment without interacting with the barista.
The customer goes to the barista and places an order for a coffee. The barista prepares the coffee and hands it back to the customer.

questions.explanation: This scenario accurately describes the client-server model. The client makes a request to the server, and the server processes the request and sends back the response.

questions.text: You work for a local charity organization. Your organization has sensitive data that must remain within your country for compliance reasons. However, you also need a solution that can scale quickly to handle seasonal spikes in demand. You decide to keep on-premises resources for compliance and use cloud-based resources for dynamic scaling. Which type of cloud deployment does this situation describe?
questions.explanation: With a hybrid cloud solution, you can keep sensitive data on-premises to meet compliance requirements while using cloud-based resources for dynamic scaling.

questions.text: A retail business plans to launch a new line of clothing, but they are struggling with accurately predicting how much server capacity they will need to support the launch. Which benefit of the AWS Cloud is most relevant to this situation?

options.text: Stop spending money to run and maintain data centers.
Trade upfront expense for variable expense.

questions.explanation: With the AWS Cloud, the company can conveniently scale resources up or down based on actual demand, eliminating the need to guess future capacity requirements.

questions.text: You just joined a tech start-up, and the business is growing rapidly. Your new company decides that they need to design a resilient and scalable infrastructure on AWS to handle increased traffic and help ensure high availability. Which statement BEST describes the AWS Global Infrastructure benefit of high availability?

options.text: AWS stores all of your website's data in a single AWS storage bucket to centralize data management.
AWS has many customer support options to make sure the answers to your questions are highly available on its website.
AWS provides multiple data centers across different geographic regions so your website can remain operational even if one location faces issues.
AWS offers a single, highly secure data center that can handle all your traffic.

questions.explanation: AWS provides multiple data centers across different geographic regions. This setup enhances high availability by making sure that your online store can handle increased traffic even during unforeseen events.

questions.text: You work for a startup company that is developing an application in the cloud. A new security update is available for your operating system (OS), and you are tasked with verifying that the OS is patched accordingly. Which statement BEST describes which party is responsible for applying security patches to the OS that is running in the cloud?

options.text: AWS is responsible for applying security patches to the OS.
Your company is responsible for applying security patches to the OS.
Both AWS and the customer apply separate patches.
The OS vendor applies the patches.

questions.explanation: The customer is responsible for managing the security configurations and patches for the OS running in the cloud.

questions.text: A finance company is interested in migrating to the cloud. They are curious if they will have enough staff to secure both their office space and the physical infrastructure of their cloud computing resources. Which statement BEST describes the company's responsibility when it comes to securing physical infrastructure of the cloud?

options.text: The company is fully responsible for securing all physical infrastructure of the cloud, including data centers.
The company does not have any responsibility when it comes to security in the cloud.
AWS is responsible for securing the physical infrastructure, and the company can focus on securing their data and applications within the cloud.
The company shares responsibility with the cloud provider for securing the physical infrastructure.

questions.explanation: AWS is responsible for the physical security of data centers, including access controls, surveillance, and environmental controls. Companies migrating to the cloud still have responsibilities such as security configurations and data within their cloud environments.

questions.text: You are a software developer working for a company that is planning to migrate its existing application to the cloud. Your team lead has asked you to explain the client-server model to the rest of the team and how cloud computing fits into this model. Which statement BEST describes the client-server model and its relationship to cloud computing?

options.text: In the client-server model, the client is responsible for securing their data, and the server is responsible for securing the physical hardware. Cloud computing replaces the client-server model.
In the client-server model, the server only handles data storage, and the client performs all processing tasks. Cloud computing provides data storage, but not processing power.
The client-server model is outdated and no longer used in modern applications. Cloud computing has replaced it completely.

In the client-server model, the client sends requests to the server, which processes the requests and sends back responses. Cloud computing provides scalable server resources that can be accessed over the internet.

questions.explanation: This accurately describes the client-server model where the client sends requests to the server. The server processes these requests and returns the appropriate responses. Cloud computing provides scalable and flexible server resources that can be accessed over the internet.

questions.text: You work for a sports apparel company that is expanding its operations to global customers. The company wants to deploy resources to multiple AWS locations to achieve high availability. Which statement BEST describes the difference between AWS Regions and Availability Zones?

options.text: A Region is a single data center, and an Availability Zone consists of multiple data centers.
An Availability Zone is a geographical location that contains three or more Regions. A Region is a distinct location within an Availability Zone that contains one or more discrete data centers.
An Availability Zone spans multiple physical locations across Regions, and Regions are located across the world.
A Region is a geographical location that contains three or more Availability Zones. An Availability Zone is a distinct location within a Region that contains one or more discrete data centers.

questions.explanation: A Region is a physical location around the world that contains multiple, isolated data centers. An Availability Zone consists of one or more discrete data centers, each with independent power, networking, and connectivity.

questions.text: A large enterprise is looking to lower operational costs. They are hoping to reduce the operational overhead associated with managing their own physical infrastructure. Which choice BEST describes which advantage of the cloud this enterprise can benefit from?

options.text: Benefit from massive economies of scale
Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers

questions.explanation: By moving to the cloud, the enterprise can offload the responsibility of maintaining physical data centers, reducing operational overhead.

questions.text: A government agency wants to maintain complete control over its IT infrastructure, but plans to use AWS Cloud services for specific applications. Which deployment type is BEST for this situation?
questions.explanation: Hybrid deployment is a solution where the agency can maintain control over on-premises systems while using cloud services for other applications.

questions.text: A small business is considering migrating their IT infrastructure to the cloud. The finance manager says they like the idea of the reliability that comes with the cloud, but they are worried about the fixed costs. Which statement accurately defines the cloud computing model and addresses the finance manager's concern?

options.text: Cloud computing involves purchasing dedicated servers and setting up a private data center. This model often comes with fixed costs related to hardware and maintenance.

Cloud computing is a model for delivering IT resources over the internet. Businesses can rent services on a pay-as-you-go basis, which can help reduce fixed costs and allow for more flexible budgeting.
Cloud computing reduces the need for additional hardware but still involves significant fixed monthly fees.
Cloud computing is basically just using someone else's server to host your website, and it does not offer any significant cost advantages over traditional hosting solutions.

questions.explanation: Cloud computing can be defined as the on-demand delivery of IT resources over the internet with pay-as-you-go pricing. This answer accurately defines cloud computing and directly addresses the finance manager's concern about fixed costs.

questions.text: Your company is planning to launch a new web application that will serve users around the world. The IT team plans to use AWS for its vast global infrastructure. What BEST describes the benefits of AWS Global Infrastructure related to this situation? _(Select TWO.)_
questions.explanation: Global infrastructure involves distributing resources across multiple data centers in different geographic locations. Redundant systems in various locations enhance fault tolerance — if one component fails, others take over to minimize downtime and ensure high availability. Ease of use, compliance, and cost savings are cloud benefits, but are not the focus here.

questions.text: You are a cloud architect at a company that has recently migrated its infrastructure to AWS. Your manager has asked you to debrief your team on which responsibilities the customer has when it comes to securing cloud resources. What is the customer's responsibility, based on the AWS Shared Responsibility Model? _(Select TWO.)_

options.text: Updating compute, networking, storage, and database software

questions.explanation: The customer is responsible for managing OS patches and encrypting client-side data. Physical security of data centers, hardware maintenance, and updating software for compute, networking, storage, and database services are all AWS responsibilities.

questions.text: How does Amazon EC2 compare to running servers on premises?

options.text: It is more expensive but offers more control.
It is more flexible, cost-effective, and quicker to get started.
It requires more time to set up and maintain.
It is only useful for large businesses.

questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 is designed to be flexible, cost-effective, and quick to set up compared to on-premises servers.

questions.text: What is multi-tenancy in the context of Amazon EC2 instances?

options.text: Each virtual machine is isolated but shares resources from a host machine.
Multiple servers run in the same data center.
Only one user can use the instance at a time.
A server can only run one type of application.

questions.explanation: Multi-tenancy allows multiple virtual machines to share resources on the same physical host, with isolation between them.

questions.text: A company uses Amazon EC2 instances to deploy their application. What would happen if they need to stop or terminate the EC2 instances?

options.text: You are still charged for the instances.
The instances will automatically restart after a set time.
You pay only for running instances, not for stopped or terminated ones.
Your instances are permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

questions.explanation: With Amazon EC2, you only pay for running instances, not for ones that are stopped or terminated.

questions.text: A financial institution is running a real-time analytics application that processes large datasets stored across multiple servers to provide quick query results. The application requires fast processing of data with a focus on handling large volumes of information efficiently. Which Amazon EC2 instance type would be the BEST choice?
questions.explanation: Memory optimized instances are designed for high-memory workloads and offer the performance needed to handle large volumes of data efficiently, making them the best choice for real-time analytics.

questions.text: A retail company is setting up a solution to analyze historical sales data that is stored locally. The solution requires fast access to large datasets with consistent, high disk throughput for quick data retrieval. Which Amazon EC2 instance type would be the MOST suitable?
questions.explanation: Storage optimized instances are designed for workloads that require high-disk throughput and low-latency access to large datasets, making them the best choice for data analytics applications.

questions.text: What must be specified when preparing to launch an Amazon EC2 instance?

options.text: The type of instance and the operating system
The amount of storage and the number of users
The instance's location and backup plan
The network bandwidth and data transfer limit

questions.explanation: You need to choose the EC2 instance type (how powerful you want it) and the Amazon Machine Image (AMI), which determines the operating system and software for your instance.

questions.text: What is a primary advantage of using the AWS Command Line Interface (AWS CLI) over the AWS Management Console?

options.text: It provides a visual, drag-and-drop interface for managing AWS resources.
It uses automation and scripting, which reduces manual steps and errors.
It is only available for Windows users.
It requires fewer configurations and is ideal for one-time manual provisioning.

questions.explanation: The AWS CLI enables automation through scripting, which is more efficient and reduces manual errors compared to the console.

questions.text: What is the customer's responsibility when using compute services like Amazon EC2 according to the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?

options.text: AWS manages the security of the cloud, and the customer manages the security of the infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for securing the physical hardware of their EC2 instances.
AWS is responsible for managing the operating system, networking, and applications on the customer's EC2 instances.
The customer is responsible for configuring, securing, and managing the operating system, networking, and applications on their EC2 instances.

questions.explanation: The customer handles the operating system, networking, and applications on the EC2 instances. AWS manages the hardware.

questions.text: What are the required configurations when launching an Amazon EC2 instance for a web server? _(Select THREE.)_
questions.explanation: To launch an EC2 instance for a web server, configure the AMI (OS and software), select the instance type (CPU, memory, storage), and set up storage options. Load balancing, permissions, and instance termination behavior are not required when launching a basic EC2 web server.

questions.text: What is an Amazon Machine Image (AMI) used for when launching an Amazon EC2 instance?

options.text: To pre-configure the operating system and software

questions.explanation: An AMI is a pre-configured virtual machine image that contains the operating system, application server, and applications, helping to launch EC2 instances quickly with the desired software and settings.

questions.text: A financial services company needs to run sensitive applications that handle confidential customer data and require compliance with industry regulations. They need complete control over the physical server, including instance placement and resource allocation. Which pricing option should they choose?
questions.explanation: Dedicated Hosts provide exclusive use of a physical server, providing full control over placement and resource allocation. This is ideal for security-sensitive and licensing-specific workloads.

questions.text: A startup is running a batch processing workload that can tolerate occasional interruptions, and they want to reduce costs by taking advantage of unused Amazon EC2 capacity. Which pricing option would offer them the most savings?
questions.explanation: Spot Instances offer up to 90% off On-Demand pricing, making them the most cost-effective option for workloads that can tolerate interruptions.

questions.text: A customer is building a new application and is unsure of their usage patterns but expects to grow and stabilize usage over time. They want to start without a long-term commitment. Which pricing option should they use?
questions.explanation: On-Demand pricing is ideal for starting without commitment. The customer can test and adjust based on actual usage before committing to longer-term options.

questions.text: What is the primary benefit of scalability and elasticity in AWS?

options.text: The ability to manually adjust resources based on peak usage
The ability to grow and shrink resources dynamically based on real-time demand
The ability to create fixed resources that never change in size
The ability to permanently increase resource capacity for long-term growth

questions.explanation: By using scalability and elasticity, businesses can adjust resources as needed for optimal performance and cost-efficiency.

questions.text: What is the main reason for deploying Amazon EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones?

options.text: To increase the power and speed of each individual instance
To provide high availability by allowing instances in different Availability Zones to handle traffic if one Availability Zone fails
To decrease the cost of instances by distributing them evenly across AWS Regions
To automatically scale instances based on resource usage in each Availability Zone

questions.explanation: By deploying instances across multiple Availability Zones, businesses can avoid service disruption in the event of an Availability Zone failure.

questions.text: How does AWS make sure that a business can meet fluctuating demand without over-provisioning resources?

options.text: By providing fixed resources that are always available
By allowing businesses to provision resources that automatically scale based on demand
By requiring businesses to purchase excess resources in advance to handle peak demand
By offering resources that are always running, regardless of demand

questions.explanation: The AWS scalability and elasticity features automatically adjust resources to meet demand, resulting in cost-efficiency and performance.

questions.text: How does Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) improve scalability in AWS?

options.text: It manually adjusts the number of Amazon EC2 instances based on traffic.
It automatically routes traffic to instances based on various routing methods.
It directly increases the size of Amazon EC2 instances.
It creates new Amazon EC2 instances for each request.

questions.explanation: ELB improves scalability by automatically distributing traffic to the EC2 instances with the least load. ELB scales elastically, adjusting traffic routing based on demand without increasing hourly costs.

questions.text: Which task does Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) perform?

options.text: Automatically adjusts the number of Amazon EC2 instances to match demand.
Distributes a workload across several Amazon EC2 instances.
Removes unneeded Amazon EC2 instances when demand is low.
Adds a second Amazon EC2 instance during an online store's popular sale.

questions.explanation: ELB distributes incoming traffic evenly across multiple EC2 instances so no single instance becomes overwhelmed. Auto Scaling adjusts the number of instances based on demand. Together, they maintain application reliability and cost efficiency.

questions.text: What BEST describes the key difference between tightly coupled and loosely coupled architectures?

options.text: In a tightly coupled architecture, components are tightly connected and dependent on each other, whereas in a loosely coupled architecture, components can operate independently.
Tightly coupled systems are more flexible in adding new components, whereas loosely coupled systems require careful configuration to add new components.
Tightly coupled architectures are designed for scalability, whereas loosely coupled systems focus on maintaining high availability.
Loosely coupled systems require components to share data directly with each other, whereas tightly coupled systems store data in a central repository.

questions.explanation: In tightly coupled systems, components are heavily interdependent — if one fails, it can cause cascading failures. In loosely coupled systems, components operate independently, so the failure of one does not disrupt the entire system.

questions.text: In a banking system, when customers transfer money, the transaction details are sent from the transaction service to a fraud detection service for verification. Sometimes, the fraud detection service is temporarily down. What is the MAIN advantage of using Amazon SQS in this scenario?

options.text: It guarantees immediate approval of transactions.
It stores transaction details until the fraud detection service can process them, even if the service is down.
It speeds up transaction processing by avoiding the use of a buffer.
It forces the transaction service and fraud detection service to depend on each other directly.

questions.explanation: Amazon SQS safely stores transaction details until the fraud detection service can process them, preventing data loss when the service is unavailable.

questions.text: What is the primary role of an Amazon Machine Image (AMI) when scaling applications?

options.text: It provides a consistent image to launch new instances.
It stores backup copies of application data.
It configures scaling policies for applications.

questions.explanation: AMIs launch new Amazon EC2 instances with the same software configuration and settings, providing consistency.

questions.text: Which option BEST describes how compute resources are provisioned and managed in the cloud?

options.text: Resources are provisioned based on demand, allowing for scaling and management.
Compute resources require constant manual management.
Resources must be pre-purchased and installed onsite.
Compute resources are fixed and cannot be scaled.

questions.explanation: Cloud computing resources are dynamic and can be adjusted in real time to meet changing demand, allowing for easier scaling and management.

questions.text: A startup company is building a new web application and chooses general purpose Amazon EC2 instances to host the application. Why did they choose this instance type?

options.text: It provides the highest performance for compute-heavy tasks, such as video rendering or machine learning.
It provides a balanced mix of compute, memory, and networking resources while keeping costs efficient as they scale their user base.
It is designed for high storage capacity, which is needed to host large databases.
It is specifically designed for memory-intensive applications, which is ideal for hosting a web application.

questions.explanation: General purpose instances offer a good balance of resources for various types of workloads, including web applications, making them a flexible and cost-effective choice.

questions.text: Which tool can be used to interact with AWS services through a graphical user interface (GUI)?
questions.explanation: The AWS Management Console is the web-based GUI used to interact with AWS services.

questions.text: A university research team is running climate modeling simulations that require substantial CPU power to process complex algorithms and analyze large datasets. Why are compute optimized Amazon EC2 instances ideal for this task?

options.text: They provide high storage throughput, making them ideal for tasks that require fast data retrieval and storage for large datasets.
They are designed for workloads that require a high amount of memory to process large datasets efficiently.
They are optimized for handling tasks that require hardware accelerators.
They are ideal for tasks that require significant CPU power to perform computations.

questions.explanation: Compute optimized instances are specifically designed for compute-heavy workloads like simulations, offering higher CPU performance to process complex algorithms and analyze large datasets.

questions.text: How do Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling and Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) work together in AWS?

options.text: ELB automatically scales the EC2 instances, and Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adjusts traffic.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adjusts the number of instances, whereas ELB distributes traffic evenly across them.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling handles traffic routing, whereas ELB manages instance scaling.
They operate independently and are not connected.

questions.explanation: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of EC2 instances in response to changes in demand. ELB distributes incoming traffic evenly across those instances, supporting high availability and reliability.

questions.text: A manufacturing company is running a set of predictable workloads for the next 3 years and wants to optimize costs. Which option should they choose?
questions.explanation: Reserved Instances offer up to a 75% discount for steady-state, predictable workloads when committing to a 1-year or 3-year term, making them the most cost-effective option.

questions.text: What does multi-tenancy refer to in the context of Amazon EC2?

options.text: EC2 instances dedicated to individual clients
Instances that are restricted to one geographical region
Each EC2 instance running in its own private network
Multiple users sharing the same EC2 instance while maintaining isolation

questions.explanation: Multi-tenancy in Amazon EC2 refers to multiple tenants sharing the same instance but maintaining isolation for security and performance.

questions.text: How does Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling work in AWS?

options.text: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adjusts the instance type but does not change the number of instances.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling adds instances when the workload is too high, but it does not scale down when demand decreases.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adds or removes instances based on performance data and application metrics.
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling requires manual intervention to add or remove instances based on traffic.

questions.explanation: For optimal resource usage, Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling automatically adjusts the number of instances based on performance metrics.

questions.text: A media company is working on a project that involves rendering complex visual effects and simulations. The rendering process requires significant computational power and hardware accelerators. Which Amazon EC2 instance type would be the BEST choice?
questions.explanation: Accelerated computing instances are equipped with GPUs and hardware accelerators, making them ideal for rendering tasks that require high computational power and efficiency.

questions.text: A software development company needs to notify the engineering team whenever a new bug is reported. Some team members need to be notified immediately, whereas others can process the bug reports later. Which service should they choose?
questions.explanation: Amazon SNS is the better choice because it delivers notifications in real time to multiple recipients simultaneously, suiting the need for immediate notifications to engineers about new bugs.

questions.text: A marketing agency is developing a new web application and expects steady growth, but is unsure of traffic in the early months. They want flexibility without a long-term commitment. Which pricing option should they use?
questions.explanation: On-Demand pricing is ideal for starting without commitment. The customer can test and adjust based on actual usage before committing to longer-term options.

questions.text: What happens if one component fails in a loosely coupled architecture?

options.text: The system can continue to function as other components are independent.
Other components must be manually restarted.
The failure causes the entire system to fail.
The system automatically switches to backup components without any intervention.

questions.explanation: Loosely coupled systems are designed so that components are independent. If one component fails, the rest of the system can continue to function normally, minimizing the impact of the failure.

questions.text: A company has critical steady-state workloads and batch jobs that are not time-sensitive. How should they optimize costs using Amazon EC2 Savings Plans and Spot Instances?

options.text: Use Savings Plans for critical workloads and Spot Instances for jobs that are not time-sensitive to maximize savings.
Use Spot Instances for all workloads to maximize savings and avoid Savings Plans.
Use Savings Plans for non-time-sensitive workloads and Spot Instances for critical jobs.
Use Savings Plans for all workloads and avoid Spot Instances because of interruption risks.

questions.explanation: Savings Plans are ideal for critical workloads that need consistent capacity and predictable pricing. Spot Instances offer significant savings for jobs that are not time-sensitive and can tolerate interruptions.

questions.text: A development team at a fintech startup is building a new customer-facing microservice using AWS Lambda. AWS takes care of the infrastructure, but the team is focused on meeting strict security and compliance requirements. Which task is still the team's responsibility when using Lambda?
questions.explanation: Although AWS manages the infrastructure, the team is responsible for correctly configuring IAM roles and permissions for any services that the Lambda function accesses.

questions.text: A photography company uses AWS Lambda to automatically process photos every time a client uploads a new image. Which key components are involved in running an automated photo processing workflow with Lambda? _(Select THREE.)_
questions.explanation: The key components of AWS Lambda are the function, triggers, and runtimes. These handle code, respond to events, and provide the language environment. Customers do not need to manage servers, scaling, or operating systems — AWS handles all of that.

questions.text: A developer's application runs flawlessly on their laptop but crashes when deployed in a different environment because of missing dependencies and configuration mismatches. How do containers help solve this problem?

options.text: By providing access to multiple operating systems for compatibility testing
By packaging the application and everything it needs to run into one consistent environment
By automatically scaling the application across multiple cloud providers
By eliminating the need to write configuration files

questions.text: A startup is building a web application and wants a simple way to deploy code without managing infrastructure. They need auto scaling and monitoring handled for them. Which AWS service should they use?
questions.explanation: Elastic Beanstalk handles deployment, scaling, and monitoring for web applications with minimal setup.

questions.text: A financial company must run workloads on premises because of strict compliance requirements, but wants to use AWS services and tools consistently across environments. Which service meets their needs?
questions.explanation: Outposts extends AWS infrastructure to on-premises environments with consistent tools and APIs.

questions.text: A development team's application runs fine locally but fails in staging because of missing dependencies and configuration mismatches. Which solution should the team use to ensure the application runs the same across all environments?

options.text: Package the application in a container that includes all dependencies.
Use Amazon EC2 to manually recreate the development environment.
Set up separate environments for each operating system.
Use different versions of the applications for different environments.

questions.explanation: Containers bundle the application, runtime, and dependencies together, providing consistency across all environments from development to production.

questions.text: A development team has stored their hotel booking system's container image in Amazon ECR and is ready to deploy it. They need a service that can automatically start and stop containers based on traffic, scale with demand, and monitor system health. Which service does the team need next?

options.text: A virtual machine to run the container manually
An orchestration service like Amazon ECS or Amazon EKS

questions.explanation: Amazon ECS or Amazon EKS can automatically scale containers, handle health checks, and manage their lifecycle based on traffic demand.

questions.text: A company is launching a containerized photo application. The container image needs to be stored securely, they plan to use Kubernetes for orchestration, and they prefer not to manage any servers. Which combination of AWS services BEST fits their needs?

options.text: Amazon S3, Amazon ECS, and Amazon EC2
Amazon ECR, Amazon ECS, and Amazon EC2
Amazon ECR, AWS Lambda, and Amazon EC2
Amazon ECR, Amazon EKS, and AWS Fargate

questions.explanation: Amazon ECR stores container images, Amazon EKS handles Kubernetes orchestration, and AWS Fargate runs containers without server management.

questions.text: A pharmaceutical research company needs to run thousands of parallel simulations to analyze protein folding. The jobs are compute-heavy, do not require real-time interaction, and should be automatically scheduled and scaled. Which AWS service BEST fits this workload?
questions.explanation: AWS Batch is designed for large-scale batch workloads and can automatically manage and scale compute resources for job queues.

questions.text: A developer is launching a new microservice and wants to focus only on writing and deploying code. They do not want to manage servers, handle scaling, or worry about infrastructure availability. Which AWS service model is BEST for this use case?
questions.explanation: With serverless services, customers can focus solely on writing and deploying code. AWS fully manages infrastructure, scaling, and availability.

questions.text: Which scenario is the BEST fit for using AWS Lambda?

options.text: Automatically processing images as users upload them to an Amazon S3 bucket
Hosting a high-traffic web server that requires full control over the OS
Running a relational database that needs complex queries and frequent connections
Running a large, high-performance computing workload that takes several hours to complete

questions.explanation: Lambda is perfect for this event-driven use case. It can run code in response to uploads and scale automatically based on the number of events.

questions.text: What is the customer responsible for managing in a serverless service like AWS Lambda?

options.text: The physical servers and networking hardware
The operating system, network configuration, and application stack

questions.explanation: With serverless services like Lambda, customers are responsible for the application code. AWS manages the infrastructure, scaling, and availability.

questions.text: A freelance developer is building a blog for a client with minimal traffic. They want a basic, cost-effective solution that includes storage and compute in one package, without complex configurations or scaling concerns. Which AWS service is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation: Lightsail is great for small projects like blogs and basic websites, offering predictable pricing and ease of use.

questions.text: A cloud engineer for a government agency is tasked with selecting an AWS Region to deploy the agency's resources. Which factors are MOST important to consider? _(Select TWO.)_

options.text: Any regulatory compliance standards the agency requires
Personal preference of the chief information officer
How recently the Region was constructed

questions.explanation: When working for a government agency, ensuring the selected Region complies with all relevant government regulations and policies is important — including data sovereignty, privacy laws, and other compliance requirements. Proximity to users also reduces latency and improves application performance.

questions.text: A rapidly growing tech startup is planning to launch a new web application requiring a complex infrastructure setup — multiple EC2 instances, Elastic Load Balancing, and Auto Scaling groups — deployed consistently across different environments. Would AWS CloudFormation be a good solution?

options.text: CloudFormation would not be useful because it only supports simple infrastructure setups.
CloudFormation would be ideal because it supports IaC, enabling consistent, repeatable deployments across different environments.
CloudFormation should be used only for static websites, not for complex applications.
CloudFormation is too complicated and would slow down the deployment process.

questions.explanation: CloudFormation is designed to handle complex infrastructure setups. It defines infrastructure as code to ensure deployments are consistent across development, testing, and production environments.

questions.text: A biomedical tech team needs to deploy a medical application across multiple AWS Regions with high availability and fault tolerance. The deployment must be consistent, repeatable, and minimize manual errors through automation. Which approach is BEST suited?

options.text: Using an IaC service such as AWS CloudFormation to deploy the application
Combining console and programmatic approaches for different tiers of the application
Writing a script to programmatically deploy the application using the cloud provider's API
Using the AWS Management Console to manually deploy each component in every Region

questions.explanation: With an IaC service like CloudFormation, the team can define their infrastructure in a declarative way, enabling version control, review, and automated deployment for consistency and repeatability.

questions.text: Which answer BEST describes the purpose and benefits of AWS edge locations?

options.text: Edge locations are used exclusively for running compute-intensive applications and databases.
Edge locations are composed of physical data centers that host the majority of AWS services and applications.
Edge locations cache content to deliver data with lower latency and higher transfer speeds to end users.
Edge locations are intended to provide redundant storage solutions for critical data.

questions.explanation: Edge locations improve content delivery by caching it closer to end users, reducing latency and increasing transfer speeds.

questions.text: What BEST describes key benefits of using multiple AWS Regions and multiple Availability Zones? _(Select TWO.)_
questions.explanation: Using multiple Regions places resources closer to end users, reducing data travel distance and lowering latency. Using multiple Availability Zones enhances fault tolerance — if one zone fails, the application continues to operate from another, maintaining high availability.

questions.text: A nonprofit organization is migrating to the AWS Cloud and trying to decide which Region to deploy their resources to. Which option lists the factors they need to consider?

options.text: Compliance, proximity to customers, feature availability, and pricing
Software licensing, hardware specifications, cloud skillset of staff, and customer reviews
Server uptime, application performance, employee satisfaction, and office location
Network speed, data encryption, user interface, and storage capacity

questions.explanation: Compliance ensures legal and regulatory requirements are met. Proximity reduces latency and improves user experience. Feature availability ensures required AWS services are supported. Pricing varies by Region, making cost-efficiency a critical factor.

questions.text: Which answer BEST describes the relationship between AWS Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations?

options.text: AWS Regions contain multiple edge locations, and each edge location can have one or more Availability Zones.
AWS Regions, Availability Zones, and edge locations are all the same thing and are used interchangeably.
AWS Regions are physical locations around the world where AWS has multiple Availability Zones. Edge locations are located outside of AWS Regions and cache frequently accessed content.
AWS Regions are physical locations around the world where AWS has multiple Availability Zones. Each Availability Zone has one or more edge locations.

questions.explanation: Regions are physical locations that contain multiple isolated Availability Zones. Edge locations are part of the AWS CDN — optimized for content delivery and not directly tied to Availability Zones.

questions.text: Which answer describes the key features and benefits of AWS CloudFormation?

options.text: With CloudFormation, users can model and set up their AWS resources using code to automate provisioning and the management of infrastructure. This method can help to reduce errors and maintain consistency across environments.
CloudFormation provides a visual interface for manually configuring each AWS resource one by one, without the need for any code.
CloudFormation is a cost-optimization tool that analyzes AWS usage and recommends best practices for reducing AWS bills.
With CloudFormation, users can cache frequently accessed content so users experience lower latency when accessing cloud resources.

questions.explanation: CloudFormation lets you model and set up resources using code, automate provisioning, reduce errors, and maintain consistency across environments.

questions.text: What is networking in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: Logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define
System that translates readable domain names to IP addresses
Interconnected devices that can exchange data and resources
Physically isolated data centers or set of data centers within an AWS Region

questions.explanation: Networking refers to interconnected devices that can exchange data and resources. The other options describe a VPC, DNS, and Availability Zones respectively.

questions.text: What are the uses of a subnet in an Amazon VPC? _(Select THREE.)_

options.text: Can be used to share public resources
Can be used to organize your resources
Can be used to provide high availability because they consist of one or more discrete data centers
Can provide a centralized interface for accessing and managing various AWS resources and services
Can be used to isolate resources and keep them private
Can be used as on-demand and scalable computing capacity in the AWS Cloud

questions.text: A company is setting up their Amazon VPC. They need to connect their corporate data center through the internet with a secure connection. They also want to make sure the resources are isolated from the public. Which solution would BEST meet their needs?

options.text: An internet gateway with a public subnet holding the resources in the Amazon VPC
A virtual private gateway with a VPN connection and a private subnet in the Amazon VPC
An internet gateway with an Amazon VPC and no subnets
An internet gateway with an Amazon VPC and a public subnet

questions.explanation: A virtual private gateway with a VPN connection provides the secure connection, and a private subnet keeps resources isolated from the public.

questions.text: A company is conducting a large-scale migration of their on-premises data center with their data warehouse and data backup. They need a solution that will meet the large amount of bandwidth requirements during migration and for ongoing data transfers in a hybrid cloud solution. Which AWS solution would best meet their needs?

options.text: AWS Direct Connect link to their on-premises network and the AWS Cloud
AWS PrivateLink to their on-premises datacenter

questions.explanation: Direct Connect would provide the bandwidth requirements for the migration, in addition to the ongoing data transfers in their hybrid solution.

questions.text: A company needs a gateway to connect their corporate data center with their private subnet in their Amazon VPC. The gateway needs to allow only protected internet traffic to enter the Amazon VPC and allow connections only from approved networks. Which type of gateway would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: A virtual private gateway allows only protected internet traffic to enter the VPC, restricting access appropriately.

questions.text: A retail customer needs a lot of control in defining traffic rules for the individual Amazon EC2 instances in their public subnet. Which solution would BEST meet the requirements for securing the resources?

options.text: Change all the subnets to private subnets to avoid accessing from the public internet.
Set up security groups for the EC2 instances based on the application requirements.
Set up network ACLs for the EC2 instances based on the application requirements.
Set up a public subnet with strong passwords that meet the customer's requirements.

questions.explanation: Security groups allow fine-grained control at the instance level.

questions.text: Which network component performs stateless packet filtering?
questions.explanation: Network ACLs are stateless and don't remember previous decisions made for incoming packets.

questions.text: A media company wants to manage all their existing domain names from different registration companies and register new domain names — all in a single service. Which AWS service would BEST fit their need?
questions.explanation: Amazon Route 53 is a highly available and scalable cloud DNS service that makes it possible to manage all domains in a single service.

questions.text: An enterprise customer with a worldwide sales force wants to deliver a large library of media-rich sales training content with low latency and reduced costs. Which AWS service would BEST fit their need?
questions.explanation: CloudFront uses the AWS global network to deliver content with low latency and high transfer speeds through a worldwide network of edge locations.

questions.text: A healthcare company needs a dedicated network connection to AWS for heavy data payloads and has compliance requirements due to the sensitive nature of patient data. Which type of connection would BEST meet their needs?

options.text: Standard internet connection from the client to an internet gateway in the VPC
VPN connection from their corporate data center to the AWS Cloud
VPN connection from their corporate data center to a virtual private gateway to their Amazon VPC
AWS Direct Connect connection from their corporate data center to a virtual private gateway in their VPC

questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides both the bandwidth for large data payloads and the security requirements needed for compliance.

questions.text: A financial customer needs a content delivery solution to deliver training videos and static content to financial consultants worldwide with low latency. Which AWS solution would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: CloudFront speeds up distribution of static and dynamic web content through a worldwide network of edge locations, delivering content with low latency.

questions.text: A customer is creating application resources in their VPC subnets and wants to secure networking traffic protection tasks. Which component is the customer responsible for, based on the shared responsibility model?

options.text: Securing the hardware that runs the AWS Cloud services
Securing the software that runs the AWS Cloud services
Securing access to the AWS data centers and facilities
Securing network traffic with the subnets and resources with network ACLs and security groups

questions.explanation: The customer is responsible for securing network traffic using security groups and network ACLs.

questions.text: A media company needs a service to manage domain registrations with different providers and route internet traffic to resources hosted both in the AWS Cloud and elsewhere. Which AWS solution would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: Route 53 manages domain registration, DNS routing, and health checks, and provides reliable routing to applications whether hosted within AWS or elsewhere.

questions.text: What is the primary function of a domain name service (DNS)?

options.text: It filters inbound and outbound traffic to Amazon EC2 instances in a VPC.
It translates human-readable domain names to machine-readable IP addresses.
It provisions a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch resources in a virtual network.
It allows you to create a subsection of a VPC where you can isolate resources and control access.

questions.explanation: A DNS translates the domain name in your browser and sends it to a customer DNS resolver, which then asks the company DNS server for the IP address so the user can reach the website.

questions.text: A customer wants a dedicated connection from their on-premises network to an Amazon VPC with a more consistent network experience and increased bandwidth. Which type of connection would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: Direct Connect establishes a dedicated connection from an on-premises network to one or more VPCs, reducing network costs, increasing bandwidth, and providing a more consistent experience than internet-based connections.

questions.text: What is the primary function of an Availability Zone in the AWS Cloud?

options.text: It filters inbound and outbound traffic to EC2 instances in a VPC.
It provisions a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network.
It enhances application availability and fault tolerance by allowing resources to be deployed across multiple zones.
It allows you to create a subsection of a VPC where you can isolate resources and control access.

questions.explanation: An Availability Zone is a physically isolated data center or set of data centers within an AWS Region with independent power, cooling, and networking, designed to enhance availability and fault tolerance.

questions.text: A customer wants to set up traffic control at the subnet level with broad control of traffic in and out, using both allow and deny type rules. Which solution would meet their needs?

options.text: Set up a virtual private gateway based on the application requirements.
Change the public subnet to a private subnet to avoid public internet access.
Use network access control lists (network ACLs).

questions.explanation: Network ACLs provide broad traffic control at the subnet level and support both allow and deny type rules.

questions.text: A retail customer wants to configure traffic rules for Amazon EC2 instances running in a public subnet, with multiple rules defined at the instance level. Which solution would meet their needs?

options.text: Set up network ACLs for the Amazon EC2 instances based on the application requirements.
Set up a virtual private gateway based on the application requirements.
Set up security groups for the Amazon EC2 instances based on the application requirements.
Change the public subnet to a private subnet to avoid public internet access.

questions.explanation: Security groups allow fine-grained control at the instance level and can define rules for individual instances in the public subnet.

questions.text: A customer wants to create a private segment in their Amazon VPC so that resources launched can be isolated from users on the internet. Which network component would BEST meet their needs?

options.text: An Availability Zone instead of a subnet
Either a public subnet or a private subnet would work

questions.explanation: A private subnet is a network segment within a VPC that does not have a direct route to the internet, isolating resources from public access.

questions.text: A company wants to establish a secure, private connection between their on-premises data center and their Amazon VPC to create a hybrid cloud architecture. Which component should they use?
questions.explanation: A virtual private gateway is the VPN endpoint on the AWS side, providing a secure, encrypted connection between your on-premises network and your VPC.

questions.text: A customer needs the most cost-effective way to establish secure, encrypted connections between their on-premises data centers and branch offices and their AWS services. They are not looking to increase bandwidth. Which solution would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: AWS Site-to-Site VPN creates a secure connection between your data center or branch offices and your AWS Cloud resources.

questions.text: An enterprise customer just merged with another company and needs to quickly scale and provide their new worldwide sales force with access to AWS resources. They want a fully managed service with advanced authentication for remote workers. Which solution would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: AWS Client VPN connects remote workers and on-premises networks to the cloud with advanced authentication, elastic remote access, and full management by AWS.

questions.text: A customer needs an edge networking solution to improve application availability, performance, and security. They specifically need traffic routing that accounts for endpoint health, user location, and policies when something goes wrong in one of their application's locations. Which AWS solution would BEST meet their needs?
questions.explanation: Global Accelerator improves availability and performance for global users by providing static IP addresses, directing traffic over the AWS global network, and routing to optimal endpoints based on health, user location, and policies.

questions.text: A developer is building an application that processes large amounts of data and requires high I/O performance. After processing, results are displayed to the user and do not need to be retained long-term. The current storage solution is experiencing performance bottlenecks during peak processing times. How could implementing an EC2 instance store improve performance?

options.text: EC2 instance store provides high I/O performance for temporary storage needs.
EC2 instance store reduces overall storage costs by offering the cheapest per-gigabyte storage option in AWS.
EC2 instance store provides a persistent storage solution that survives instance failures.
EC2 instance store allows storage to be conveniently shared between multiple EC2 instances simultaneously.

questions.explanation: EC2 instance store is directly attached to the host, offering extremely low latency and high I/O performance for applications that need temporary storage with fast access.

questions.text: AnyCompany Finance is designing an application that requires consistent and low-latency access to financial data and needs persistent block storage. Why would Amazon EBS be suitable?

options.text: Amazon EBS allows sharing volumes across multiple AWS Regions without any latency impact.
Amazon EBS automatically scales volume capacity without requiring any volume modifications.
Amazon EBS provides unlimited IOPS for all volume types, regardless of size.
Amazon EBS provides high availability and durability by automatically replicating volumes within the same Availability Zone.

questions.explanation: Amazon EBS ensures data protection through automatic replication within the same Availability Zone, providing the high availability and durability needed for financial applications with critical data.

questions.text: AnyCompany Software is migrating their on-premises application to AWS and is concerned about data loss when VMs crash. How does Amazon EBS solve the data persistence issue?

options.text: EBS automatically synchronizes data with Amazon S3 every 5 minutes for immediate restore.
Amazon EBS caches all data in memory for instant recovery when instances are restarted.
Amazon EBS volumes exist independently from the instance and persist even after the instance is terminated.
Amazon EBS automatically backs up all data to Amazon S3 every hour.

questions.explanation: EBS volumes have a lifecycle independent from EC2 instances. They can be detached from one instance and attached to another, and data remains intact even if the instance is shut down or terminated.

questions.text: AnyCompany Commerce's development team frequently needs to create test environments that mirror production for testing new features, but setup has become time-consuming and error-prone. What is the most significant benefit of using EBS snapshots in this scenario?

options.text: EBS snapshots enable rapid creation of new volumes from existing data, allowing quick deployment of identical test environments that mirror production.
EBS snapshots automatically optimize EBS volume performance by defragmenting data during the snapshot process.
EBS snapshots reduce storage costs by compressing all data on EBS volumes by up to 90%.
EBS snapshots automatically migrate data to different AWS Regions for improved global access speeds.

questions.explanation: EBS snapshots capture the exact state of a volume at a point in time, making them perfect for creating consistent copies of environments for testing purposes.

questions.text: AnyCompany Healthcare manages thousands of EBS volumes containing patient records and needs to ensure data is properly backed up, retained per compliance requirements, and old snapshots are removed to control costs. Which problem does AWS Data Lifecycle Manager primarily solve?

options.text: Providing real-time monitoring of EBS volume performance metrics
Encrypting sensitive patient data stored on EBS volumes
Automating the creation, retention, and deletion of EBS snapshots and EBS-backed AMIs according to a schedule
Migrating patient data from on-premises storage to AWS Cloud storage

questions.explanation: Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager defines lifecycle policies that automate snapshot management based on schedules, eliminating the need for manual snapshot management.

questions.text: AnyCompany Mobile is evaluating cloud storage solutions for their new mobile application. Based on this scenario, what is an aspect of Amazon S3 they could use?

options.text: Running relational database queries for user authentication
Hosting virtual machines to process application logic
Real-time data streaming and message processing
Storing and distributing mobile application content and user-generated media files

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is designed for scalable object storage that can efficiently store and serve various content types — ideal for mobile applications that need to store and distribute user-generated content like photos and videos.

questions.text: AnyCompany Marketing created an Amazon S3 bucket to host website images that need to be publicly accessible. After uploading, users report they cannot access the images even though the bucket policy allows public access. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The images are not properly tagged with public read permissions.
Block public access settings are enabled at the account or bucket level.
They need to enable the website hosting feature to make any Amazon S3 content public.
The S3 bucket must be in the same AWS Region as the users trying to access the content.

questions.explanation: S3 Block Public Access settings override bucket policies, preventing public access even when policies allow it.

questions.text: AnyCompany Media distributes large video files to users around the world and needs a service that is highly durable, scalable, and offers various access control mechanisms. Which Amazon S3 feature would be particularly beneficial?

options.text: Amazon S3 offers 99.999999999% (11 nines) of durability for objects stored across multiple Availability Zones, maintaining high availability for video content and protecting against data loss.
Amazon S3 automatically compresses all stored objects to reduce storage costs by up to 90%.
Amazon S3 limits individual object sizes to 5 GB, requiring companies to split large video files before upload.
Amazon S3 provides built-in video transcoding services that automatically convert videos to different formats when uploaded.

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is designed with this durability level as a key feature, which is critical for valuable media assets that cannot be conveniently recreated if lost.

questions.text: AnyCompany Business stores a growing amount of customer data in Amazon S3. Data is frequently accessed for the first 30 days, occasionally accessed for the next 60 days, and rarely accessed after 90 days. What should they do to optimize costs while maintaining appropriate access?

options.text: Create a lifecycle rule to transition objects to S3 Standard-IA after 30 days, then transition to S3 Glacier after 90 days.
Create a lifecycle rule to immediately transition all objects to S3 Glacier and set up a restore policy.
Create a lifecycle rule to delete all objects after 90 days and rely on database backups.
Create multiple S3 buckets with different storage classes and manually move objects between buckets as they age.

questions.explanation: This policy matches access patterns by using S3 Standard for frequent access, S3 Standard-IA after 30 days of inactivity, and S3 Glacier for archive after 90 days.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes S3 Lifecycle?

options.text: A backup service that creates copies of S3 objects in different AWS Regions to protect against Regional failures.
A feature that automatically compresses Amazon S3 objects after a certain period to reduce storage costs.
A monitoring system that alerts administrators when Amazon S3 objects have not been accessed for a specified period.
A feature used to define rules to automatically transition objects between different storage classes, or delete them based on age or usage patterns.

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 Lifecycle creates rules that automate the transition of objects between storage classes and set expiration dates for objects, optimizing storage costs while maintaining access based on changing data value over time.

questions.text: AnyCompany Financial needs a data application that analyzes market data and must scale compute resources up or down while maintaining access to the same datasets. What is a benefit of using Amazon EFS?

options.text: Amazon EFS provides the lowest cost storage option for infrequently accessed financial data.
Amazon EFS provides faster read access than local instance storage for high-frequency trading algorithms.
Amazon EFS provides elastic storage capacity, automatically scaling up and down as files are added and removed, with no disruption to applications.
Amazon EFS provides automatic global replication of data, eliminating the need for data backups.

questions.explanation: Amazon EFS automatically scales as data is added or removed, allowing compute resources to scale independently while maintaining access to the same elastic file system.

questions.text: AnyCompany Retail is migrating their on-premises file storage to AWS and needs to allow their global development teams to collaborate on the same set of files simultaneously. Which feature of Amazon EFS makes it a good fit?

options.text: Amazon EFS provides shared access for thousands of Amazon EC2 instances, with consistent low latencies.
Amazon EFS makes it possible to store files in the cloud, but requires manual synchronization between different instances.
Amazon EFS provides block storage that can be formatted with any file system.
Amazon EFS offers single-instance access with high throughput for dedicated processing.

questions.explanation: Amazon EFS is designed for concurrent shared access from multiple EC2 instances across different Availability Zones with consistent performance, enabling global teams to collaborate on the same files.

questions.text: AnyCompany Media wants to migrate file shares to AWS while maintaining compatibility with existing applications. They need a solution that supports SMB protocol and integrates with Microsoft Active Directory. Which AWS service should they implement?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server

questions.explanation: FSx for Windows File Server is a fully managed Windows file system that supports SMB protocol, integrates with Active Directory, and provides the Windows compatibility needed for this scenario.

questions.text: AnyCompany has a large collection of on-premises files to back up to AWS. They want to maintain local access to frequently used files while using cloud storage for cost savings, and minimize changes to existing file-sharing workflows. Which AWS Storage Gateway type BEST addresses these requirements?
questions.explanation: S3 File Gateway provides a file interface into Amazon S3 using standard file protocols like NFS and SMB. Frequently accessed data is cached locally for low-latency access while data is stored in Amazon S3 for durability and cost-effectiveness.

questions.text: AnyCompany Manufacturing stores CAD files that need frequent local access by engineers. They want to maintain local access while benefiting from AWS Cloud storage and backing up data to AWS. Which AWS Storage Gateway configuration should they choose?

options.text: Implement Storage Gateway in Tape Gateway mode to back up CAD files to virtual tapes in Amazon S3.
Implement Storage Gateway in Stored Volume mode with all volumes stored entirely in Amazon S3.
Deploy Amazon FSx for Windows File Server in the on-premises data center.
Implement Storage Gateway in Cached Volume mode to keep frequently accessed data local while storing the complete dataset in Amazon S3.

questions.explanation: Cached Volume mode stores the most frequently accessed data locally for low-latency access while maintaining the complete dataset in Amazon S3, providing both the local performance engineers need and the benefits of secure cloud backup.

questions.text: AnyCompany Telecommunications experienced an outage last quarter and wants to implement a disaster recovery solution for their on-premises server fleet with limited IT staff. The CEO wants exact replicas of production servers and minimal recovery time. What is a key benefit of using AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery?

options.text: Automated creation of separate development and test environments from production backups
Direct connection to AWS Storage Gateway to accelerate backup and recovery operations
Continuous block-level replication with frequent backup intervals, providing near-instant recovery of servers at the target AWS Region
Elimination of source-side agents by using existing backup software

questions.explanation: Elastic Disaster Recovery provides continuous block-level replication that maintains exact server replicas with minimal time between backup intervals, enabling rapid recovery when needed.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes Amazon S3?

options.text: A file storage service that provides a traditional file system interface for sharing files between multiple instances.
A scalable object storage service that is used to store and retrieve any amount of data from anywhere on the web.
A block-level storage service that provides persistent storage for Amazon EC2 instances.
A storage service primarily designed for long-term archival and backup of data that is rarely accessed.

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is fundamentally an object storage service designed for scalability. Users can store unlimited amounts of data accessible from anywhere with internet connectivity.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery?

options.text: A service that minimizes downtime and data loss by providing fast, reliable recovery of on-premises and cloud-based applications using affordable storage, minimal compute, and point-in-time recovery
A service that automatically scales computing capacity based on workload demands during disaster events
A backup service that creates periodic snapshots of AWS resources and stores them in Amazon S3 buckets
A managed database service that provides automated backups and cross-Region replication

questions.explanation: This accurately describes the core functionality of Elastic Disaster Recovery, which focuses on continuous replication to provide rapid recovery with minimal data loss.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS)?

options.text: A fully managed, elastic file system that scales automatically as files are added and removed
An object storage service designed for long-term archival of rarely accessed data
A block storage service that provides volumes to attach to a single Amazon EC2 instance
A fixed-capacity file storage system that requires manual scaling and management

questions.explanation: This accurately describes Amazon EFS as a service that automatically grows and shrinks with storage needs without manual provisioning.

questions.text: What is a key characteristic of Amazon S3 storage classes pricing?

options.text: Pricing varies based on storage costs, retrieval fees, and minimum storage durations.
Pricing is exclusively determined by the geographic region of the bucket.
All storage classes have the same minimum storage duration requirement.
All storage classes cost the same but differ in retrieval speed.

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 storage classes have different pricing structures that include storage costs per gigabyte, potential data retrieval charges, and minimum storage duration commitments.

questions.text: AnyCompany Commerce recently migrated to an Amazon EC2 instance and needs a storage solution that meets the following criteria: persistent block storage with consistent low-latency performance, attachable/detachable from EC2, independently resizable, and capable of point-in-time backups. Which AWS storage service would BEST meet the requirements?
questions.explanation: Amazon EBS provides persistent block storage volumes that can be attached to EC2 instances, offers consistent low-latency performance, can be scaled independently of the EC2 instance, and supports point-in-time snapshots for backup.

questions.text: AnyCompany Manufacturing needs to gradually migrate engineering drawings and design files to AWS while maintaining low-latency access for frequently used files and integrating with existing file-based workflows. Which AWS storage service should they use?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server

questions.explanation: File Gateway provides a file interface into Amazon S3 object storage, maintains low-latency local access to frequently accessed data through local caching, and seamlessly integrates with existing file-based workflows.

questions.text: AnyCompany Technology needs a centralized storage solution for their development team allowing multiple Amazon EC2 instances to access the same file system simultaneously. Requirements: fully managed, auto-scaling, no capacity planning, Linux support with standard file system interfaces, consistent low-latency access, and high durability. Which AWS storage service is BEST suited?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides a scalable, fully managed elastic NFS that can be accessed concurrently by multiple EC2 instances. It automatically grows as data is added and offers POSIX compliance for Linux applications.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)?

options.text: A file storage system that allows multiple instances to access the same data simultaneously
An in-memory cache used to improve database performance
A block-level storage service that provides persistent storage volumes for Amazon EC2 instances
A temporary storage option that is lost when an instance is stopped or terminated

questions.explanation: Amazon EBS is specifically designed as a block storage service offering persistent volumes that can be attached to EC2 instances and survive instance termination.

questions.text: AnyCompany Marketing needs to distribute large collections of high-resolution images, videos, and design files. Requirements: unlimited storage, high durability, easy file sharing via URLs, organization by client/project, cost-efficient storage options, and security controls. Which AWS service is BEST suited?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 provides scalable object storage ideal for storing and distributing media files, with lifecycle policies for cost optimization, bucket organization, access controls for security, and the ability to generate URLs for easy file sharing.

questions.text: AnyCompany Software needs to back up EBS volumes containing application code and customer data. Requirements: regular backups, duplicate environments for testing, disaster recovery strategy, full or incremental backups without impacting performance, and cost-effective long-term storage. Which AWS service or feature BEST meets the requirements?
questions.explanation: EBS snapshots are point-in-time copies of EBS volumes that can be used for regular backups, creating duplicate volumes for testing, and enabling fast disaster recovery by restoring entire volumes from snapshots.

questions.text: AnyCompany AI is developing a machine learning application that requires extremely fast data processing for temporary model training datasets. Requirements: highest possible I/O performance, directly attached to EC2 instances, and temporary storage that does not persist when instances are stopped or terminated. Which AWS service BEST meets these needs?
questions.explanation: EC2 instance store provides temporary block-level storage physically attached to the host computer, offering the highest possible I/O performance for temporary data processing needs.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes AWS Storage Gateway?

options.text: A migration tool that automatically transfers all on-premises data to the cloud
A hybrid cloud storage solution that provides on-premises applications with access to virtually unlimited cloud storage
A physical hardware appliance that must be installed in a data center
A virtual private network that creates secure connections between a data center and AWS

questions.explanation: Storage Gateway is fundamentally designed as a hybrid solution that connects on-premises environments to AWS Cloud storage, allowing local applications to seamlessly access AWS storage while maintaining local access patterns.

questions.text: What is the primary function of Amazon S3 storage classes?

options.text: To organize objects within buckets hierarchically
To provide different storage options optimized for different use cases and access patterns
To encrypt data at rest and in transit
To replicate data across multiple AWS Regions automatically

questions.explanation: Amazon S3 storage classes offer different performance characteristics, availability levels, and cost structures to match specific data storage requirements and access patterns.

questions.text: Which statement BEST describes Amazon FSx?

options.text: An object storage solution designed for archiving rarely accessed data
A service used to set up private file sharing within an on-premises data center only
A block storage service that provides high performance volumes for EC2 instances
A fully managed service that provides cost-effective, scalable file storage built on widely used file systems

questions.explanation: Amazon FSx is specifically designed as a fully managed service offering file storage based on popular file systems like Windows File Server, Lustre, OpenZFS, and NetApp ONTAP.

questions.text: AnyCompany Marketing needs to migrate existing file shares to AWS for design teams who work with large creative files. Requirements: fully managed, seamless Windows application integration, SMB protocol support, Active Directory integration, data deduplication, sub-millisecond latencies, and high availability. Which AWS storage service BEST meets the requirements?

options.text: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server

questions.explanation: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server is specifically designed for Windows workloads, providing SMB support, Active Directory integration, Windows features like data deduplication, and consistent sub-millisecond latencies as a fully managed service.

questions.text: AnyCompany Logistics is growing and expanding their online platform. The development team must choose between hosting their own database on Amazon EC2 or using a fully managed AWS database service. The company wants to reduce IT maintenance tasks so staff can focus more on development. Which option should they choose?

options.text: Choose the self-hosted database on Amazon EC2, because it is always more cost effective for growing companies.
Choose a managed database service, because the team wants to focus on developing new features rather than database administration tasks.
Choose the self-hosted database on Amazon EC2, because it will always provide better performance for ecommerce applications.
Choose the managed database service, because it will automatically optimize the company's application code.

questions.explanation: Fully managed AWS database services handle heavy lifting tasks like backups, patches, and high availability, letting the development team focus on building application features that drive business value.

questions.text: AnyCompany Retail wants to migrate its product database to AWS to reduce operational overhead. Their database contains critical inventory and pricing information that requires continuous operation with minimal downtime. Which feature of Amazon RDS addresses these requirements?
questions.explanation: Multi-AZ deployments provide enhanced availability and durability by automatically creating database replicas in different Availability Zones. In case of infrastructure failure, Amazon RDS performs an automatic failover to the standby instance, ensuring the database remains accessible with minimal disruption.

questions.text: AnyCompany Financial is experiencing slow response times with their MySQL database during peak transaction periods — over 100,000 transactions per hour. The CTO is looking at Amazon Aurora to significantly improve performance. Which Aurora feature BEST addresses this scenario?

options.text: Aurora provides built-in automated instance scaling that adds more CPU resources during peak transaction times.
Aurora includes data compression technology that reduces storage requirements for faster query performance.
Aurora provides a high-performance, highly available storage architecture that offers up to five times the throughput of standard MySQL.
Aurora offers a centralized storage system that eliminates network latency between instances.
